scenario.title: Celebrating a Fellowship Win
scenario.context: An academic setting where Speaker 1 and Speaker 2 are sitting at a small café near their university after a long day of classes and research. The café is buzzing with activity—students chatting, coffee machines whirring—and the smell of fresh pastries fills the air.
scenario.description: Speaker 1 has just received news that they were awarded a prestigious research fellowship. They are bubbling with excitement and joy, eager to share this moment with their friend, Speaker 2. While Speaker 1 is animated and overjoyed, Speaker 2’s reaction will test their ability to read and respond to Speaker 1’s emotional state appropriately. This scene is designed to explore how Speaker 2 manages their flexibility in adapting to Speaker 1’s elevated emotional state.
scenario.target_social_intent: Taylor must demonstrate flexibility by adapting emotionally to Speaker 1’s elevated, ecstatic state. She should amplify and celebrate Speaker 1’s joy while maintaining a positive and engaged presence.
scenario.speaker1_persona: Bubbly and ecstatic, speaking quickly and enthusiastically with energetic hand gestures and a wide smile. They feel deeply proud of their achievement and hope Speaker 2 will share in their excitement.
scenario.speaker2_persona_1: Warm and highly enthusiastic. Taylor mirrors Speaker 1’s excitement with genuine smiles, animated gestures, and words of affirmation like, 'This is amazing! You deserve it so much!' They ask follow-up questions about the fellowship to keep the conversation flowing and show engagement.
scenario.speaker2_persona_2: The Impatient Evader. Taylor seems distracted and rushed, glancing at her phone periodically. She responds with curt, uninterested phrases like, 'Oh, that’s cool,' and quickly changes the topic, showing a lack of emotional investment.
scenario.speaker2_persona_3: The Anxious Spiraler. Taylor immediately makes the situation about her own insecurities, saying things like, 'Wow, I could never get something like that! I don’t even think I’ll ever be good enough.' Her tone becomes strained, and she spirals into self-critical commentary.
scenario.speaker2_persona_high: Warm and highly enthusiastic. Taylor mirrors Speaker 1’s excitement with genuine smiles, animated gestures, and words of affirmation like, 'This is amazing! You deserve it so much!' They ask follow-up questions about the fellowship to keep the conversation flowing and show engagement.
scenario.speaker2_persona_low: The Impatient Evader. Taylor seems distracted and rushed, glancing at her phone periodically. She responds with curt, uninterested phrases like, 'Oh, that’s cool,' and quickly changes the topic, showing a lack of emotional investment.

conversation.speaker: speaker1
conversation.text: Okay, okay, Taylor, you will not believe what just happened today! Oh my gosh, I’ve been waiting to tell you all day—you're gonna burst!

conversation.speaker: speaker2
conversation.text: What?! Tell me! You’re already making me burst just watching you—spill it!

conversation.speaker: speaker1
conversation.text: I GOT THE FELLOWSHIP! The one I applied for months ago—the big one! Full funding, research support, everything! They emailed me this morning, and I almost screamed in the library!

conversation.speaker: speaker2
conversation.text: Oh my gosh, no way! That is HUGE! You totally deserve this—like, seriously, I knew it! Full funding?! Tell me everything—what did the email say? How are you feeling?

conversation.speaker: speaker1
conversation.text: I’m still shaking! It was all formal, like, ‘Congratulations, we’re thrilled to offer you—’ but I just kept re-reading it like, is this even real?! And they want me to present at the symposium next semester—Taylor, I’m gonna cry!

conversation.speaker: speaker2
conversation.text: You’re gonna absolutely kill it at the symposium—I can already picture it! Seriously, this is so incredible. You need to celebrate properly—pastry, coffee, you name it. What’s your plan?!
